name: Sand Creek Raceway
_osm_road_widths_doc: Real street circuit — after the project stage, scripts.gps.widths_from_osm map-matches each centerline vertex to its OSM way and sets curb-to-curb widths from lanes (arterials ~2x a side street) + writes intersection pads at the real junctions. Racetracks (defined widths) leave this off.

capture._doc: Sand Creek is a ROAD circuit stitched from real Commerce City streets, so its elevation is the REAL road-surface profile: USGS 3DEP sampled ALONG the real street centerline (3 m spacing), median-despiked + smoothed into a launch-free longitudinal grade, with the terrain conforming DOWN to that road. The phone's GPS/baro altitude is NOT used — it is too noisy on flat terrain and injected fake metre-scale bumps (56th/45th/Sand Creek Dr) and a phone-fell dip (~idx 1240); use_elevation=false ignores it. Captured TEXTURES and lateral evidence in realworld_capture.json ARE still consumed (do not delete the file). 'bridges' is RETAINED: evidence.level_bridge_decks uses it to hold a LEVEL deck (geometry only, no phone) over the two real Sand Creek crossings where bare-earth 3DEP dives into the creek bed — E 56th over Sand Creek (station ~2485, bare earth dips ~7 m) and Quebec over Sand Creek (station ~5950, ~6 m); both also get visual parapets/piers from build_env._creek_bridge. 'bridges' = [{center_m, len_m}] in lap-station metres.

bridges.name: e46th_leg_over_sand_creek
bridges._doc: Third crossing Kevin reported: the dip after the rise on the 46th leg - a real bridge with railing over the creek channel; the channel is NOT in OSM waterways so it was never leveled or dressed (found at 5.2 m deep in the smooth profile).

road_text._doc: Big painted street-name decals at each turn-onto. mode 'along' = lengthwise down the lane (drive over it, head-tilt to read); 'across' = sign-flat facing the driver (wraps the road type to a 2nd line). cap_m = letter height across the road. flip_read/flip_up mirror the axes if it reads backwards in-game. min_leg_m = shortest street leg that gets a label.

road_profile._doc: Corkscrew dips/cambers REMOVED — Sand Creek elevation now comes from real USGS 3DEP + capture (user: the hand-authored corkscrew sucks). Re-add dips/cambers here to bring it back.

road_edge._doc: Urban street edge swept along every road: road -> raised curb -> flat sidewalk -> graded grass as ONE continuous profile sharing seam vertices with the road and the grass, so nothing hovers (scripts/geometry/ribbon.py:curb_sidewalk; physical 1KERB_sidewalk). Omit (or profile != 'sidewalk') for the default asphalt shoulder. curb_h/sidewalk_w/grade_w in metres.

scenery._doc: Per-track foliage/scatter knobs read by scripts/environment/build_env.py. Defaults (shown) reproduce the original look; override per track (e.g. a desert track wants sparser, lower scrub). corridor_margin_m keeps ALL scatter this far clear of every road (main loop + connectors).

signs._doc: Street-name blade signs at each major junction (from data/street_labels.json, same legs as the painted road decals). enabled=false drops them. flip_read/flip_up are one-bit fixes if the text reads mirrored or upside-down in-game (verify on the first Windows drive).

forest3d._doc: 3D poplars scattered near the streets; riparian billboards stay on the creek

layouts._doc: Outer loop: Colorado -> 50th -> Dahlia -> 56th -> Sand Creek -> Quebec -> 45th -> 46th -> start. The 'interior' layout was dropped: its interior_grid connector was never defined, and layouts share one kn5 so a distinct interior circuit isn't achievable without splitting geometry.
same world, opposite direction — own grid/timing via dummies_reverse.json + spawn kn5

streetlight._doc: Wide DIM wash (Kevin: ~3x spread, no ground saturation). CSP RANGE scales deposited energy - widening range demands cutting intensity: 60 m range at 1.1, soft 155-deg cone, high gradient offset, damped specular.

kerb._about: Racing-circuit kerbs: taller + rideable ramp on the lane side (vs the default 5cm street kerb).

road_markings._doc: Lake Murray-style painted lines (double-yellow + white edges) - same assets as the Lariat.

props._doc: Kevin's model pack: 4 m concrete barrier modules instanced along warning-barrier runs as collidable 1WALL geometry (replaces the procedural swept jersey).

1ROAD._doc: Kevin: use the Lariat's road texture (stock cracked asphalt), not the phone capture

1GRASS._doc: Kevin: the Lariat's alpine grass look on Sand Creek too

LIGHTPOST._doc: the purpose-modeled lamp's own skin (emissive baked, opacity alpha)
